title: Count Square Submatrices with All 1s | DP on Rectangles : DP 56
content:
Problem Statement: Given an n * m matrix of ones and zeros, return how many square submatrices have all ones.

Examples 
Example 1:
Input: matrix = [ [0,1,1,1],
[1,1,1,1],
[0,1,1,1]]
Output: 15
Explanation: 
There are 10 squares of size 1.
There are 4 squares of size 2.
There is  1 square of size 3.
Total number of squares = 10 + 4 + 1 = 15.

Example 2:
Input: matrix =  [ [1,0,1],
[1,1,0],
[1,1,0]]
Output: 7
Explanation: 
There are 6 squares of size 1.  
There is 1 square of size 2. 
Total number of squares = 6 + 1 = 7.

Disclaimer: Here is the practice link to help you assess your knowledge better. It's highly recommend trying to solve it before looking at the solution.

Approach
Algorithm

We want to count all square submatrices in a binary matrix that are filled with 1s. Instead of brute force checking each possible square (which is expensive), we use dynamic programming to reuse results of smaller subproblems. If we know the largest square ending at a certain cell, we can build larger squares from it.

  • Create a DP table dp[n][m] where dp[i][j] represents the size of the largest square submatrix with bottom-right corner at (i, j).
  • Initialization:
    • First row and first column remain the same as the input matrix since no bigger square can be formed there.
  • Transition:
    • If arr[i][j] == 0 → no square possible → dp[i][j] = 0.
    • If arr[i][j] == 1 → dp[i][j] = 1 + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1], dp[i-1][j-1]).
      This ensures the current cell extends the smallest possible square from its neighbors.
  • Each dp[i][j] contributes its value to the total count, since if a square of size k ends here, then it contains k smaller squares within it.
Code
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344454647484950515253545556575859606162 
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

// Function to count total square submatrices with all 1s
int countSquares(int n, int m, vector<vector<int>> &arr) {
    // DP table to store size of largest square ending at (i,j)
    vector<vector<int>> dp(n, vector<int>(m, 0));

    // Initialize first row of DP table
    for (int j = 0; j < m; j++) 
        dp[0][j] = arr[0][j];

    // Initialize first column of DP table
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) 
        dp[i][0] = arr[i][0];

    // Fill the rest of the DP table
    for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
        for (int j = 1; j < m; j++) {
            // If current cell is 0, no square ends here
            if (arr[i][j] == 0) 
                dp[i][j] = 0;
            else {
                // Minimum of top, left, top-left + 1
                dp[i][j] = 1 + min(dp[i - 1][j],
                                   min(dp[i - 1][j - 1], dp[i][j - 1]));
            }
        }
    }

    // Sum up all values in DP table
    int sum = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        for (int j = 0; j < m; j++) {
            sum += dp[i][j];
        }
    }

    // Return total count of squares
    return sum;
}

int main() {
    // Input matrix
    vector<vector<int>> arr = {
        {0, 1, 1, 1}, 
        {1, 1, 1, 1},
        {0, 1, 1, 1}
    };
    
    // Matrix dimensions
    int n = 3, m = 4;

    // Call function
    int squares = countSquares(n, m, arr);

    // Print the result
    cout << "The number of squares: " << squares << "\n";

    return 0;
}

class Solution {
    // Function to count total square submatrices with all 1s
    public int countSquares(int n, int m, int[][] arr) {
        // DP table to store size of largest square ending at (i,j)
        int[][] dp = new int[n][m];

        // Initialize first row of DP table
        for (int j = 0; j < m; j++)
            dp[0][j] = arr[0][j];

        // Initialize first column of DP table
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            dp[i][0] = arr[i][0];

        // Fill the rest of the DP table
        for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
            for (int j = 1; j < m; j++) {
                // If current cell is 0, no square ends here
                if (arr[i][j] == 0)
                    dp[i][j] = 0;
                else {
                    // Minimum of top, left, top-left + 1
                    dp[i][j] = 1 + Math.min(dp[i - 1][j],
                                Math.min(dp[i - 1][j - 1], dp[i][j - 1]));
                }
            }
        }

        // Sum up all values in DP table
        int sum = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < m; j++) {
                sum += dp[i][j];
            }
        }

        // Return total count of squares
        return sum;
    }
}

public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        // Input matrix
        int[][] arr = {
            {0, 1, 1, 1},
            {1, 1, 1, 1},
            {0, 1, 1, 1}
        };

        // Matrix dimensions
        int n = 3, m = 4;

        // Create Solution object
        Solution obj = new Solution();

        // Call function
        int squares = obj.countSquares(n, m, arr);

        // Print the result
        System.out.println("The number of squares: " + squares);
    }
}

class Solution:
    # Function to count total square submatrices with all 1s
    def countSquares(self, n, m, arr):
        # DP table to store size of largest square ending at (i,j)
        dp = [[0]*m for _ in range(n)]

        # Initialize first row
        for j in range(m):
            dp[0][j] = arr[0][j]

        # Initialize first column
        for i in range(n):
            dp[i][0] = arr[i][0]

        # Fill the rest of DP table
        for i in range(1, n):
            for j in range(1, m):
                # If current cell is 0, no square ends here
                if arr[i][j] == 0:
                    dp[i][j] = 0
                else:
                    # Minimum of top, left, top-left + 1
                    dp[i][j] = 1 + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i-1][j-1], dp[i][j-1])

        # Sum up all values in DP table
        total = sum(sum(row) for row in dp)

        # Return total count of squares
        return total

# Input matrix

    [0, 1, 1, 1],
    [1, 1, 1, 1],
    [0, 1, 1, 1]
]

# Matrix dimensions
n, m = 3, 4

# Create Solution object
obj = Solution()

# Call function
squares = obj.countSquares(n, m, arr)

# Print the result
print("The number of squares:", squares)

class Solution {
    // Function to count total square submatrices with all 1s
    countSquares(n, m, arr) {
        // DP table to store size of largest square ending at (i,j)
        let dp = Array.from({length: n}, () => Array(m).fill(0));

        // Initialize first row
        for (let j = 0; j < m; j++)
            dp[0][j] = arr[0][j];

        // Initialize first column
        for (let i = 0; i < n; i++)
            dp[i][0] = arr[i][0];

        // Fill the rest of DP table
        for (let i = 1; i < n; i++) {
            for (let j = 1; j < m; j++) {
                // If current cell is 0, no square ends here
                if (arr[i][j] === 0)
                    dp[i][j] = 0;
                else
                    // Minimum of top, left, top-left + 1
                    dp[i][j] = 1 + Math.min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i-1][j-1], dp[i][j-1]);
            }
        }

        // Sum up all values in DP table
        let total = 0;
        for (let i = 0; i < n; i++)
            for (let j = 0; j < m; j++)
                total += dp[i][j];

        // Return total count of squares
        return total;
    }
}

// Input matrix
let arr = [
    [0, 1, 1, 1],
    [1, 1, 1, 1],
    [0, 1, 1, 1]
];

// Matrix dimensions
let n = 3, m = 4;

// Create Solution object
let obj = new Solution();

// Call function
let squares = obj.countSquares(n, m, arr);

// Print the result
console.log("The number of squares:", squares);
Complexity Analysis

Time Complexity: O(n × m) → each cell is processed once.

Space Complexity: O(n × m) → DP table used.
